The farmers of this country have in two years pro
duced wealth exceeding the output of all the gold mines
of the entire world since Columbus discovered America.


'This years product is six times the amount OF
the capital stock of all national banks it lacks but three
fourths of billion dollars of the value of the manufact
ures of 1900 less the cost of materials used.


cit is three times the gross earnings from the opera
tons of the railways and four times the value of all MN
erals produced in this country"


This is not page from Arabian Nights tale, but is
the cam official assertion of the Hon. Jas Wilson, secre-
tary of agriculture


In his annual report Secretary Wilson mparts the COM
forng information that the crop of 1904 alone is so
profitable that from its proceeds the farmer could have
paid the national debt and left big surplus in the treas
ury.


The hens have produced during the year and two
thirds billions of dozens of eggs-enough each month to
pay years interest the national debt.


The value of the total products of the farms for the
year estimated at $49OOOOO0OO after excluding
cent than the value of the crops of 1903 and 328
per cent above that for the census year 1899


The increase in capital invested in agriculture since
1900 estimated at $2O00OO0O0O This is exclusive of
the increase in farm land values


Bank deposits have increased from 1896 to 1904 in
Iowa 64 per cent in Kansas 219 per cent and in Missss
pp 30 per cent The general increase for the whole
try is 81 per cent


Agriculture used to be popularity looked upon the
backbone of American industry Of recent years the
nous development of trusts and the almost fabulous fig
used in connection with the railway and banking
terests have blinded to the solid industry that still fur
nishes the foundation for all other business.
